title: Session-to-Knowledge Memory Pipeline
description: Build a pipeline that extracts structured knowledge from AI session transcripts, scores entries, promotes high-value patterns to a persistent knowledge base, and injects them into agent context.

title: Foundational: Core Memory Package
description: WHY: All user stories depend on the core types, store, and scorer. These must be built first.
design: Create pkg/cli/memory/ package with types.go (KnowledgeEntry, Score, EntryStatus), store.go (JSONL CRUD), scorer.go (composite calculation).

title: Define KnowledgeEntry types and constants
description: WHY: All other code depends on the core types for knowledge entries, scores, and statuses.
design: File: pkg/cli/memory/types.go. Define KnowledgeEntry struct (id, title, description, tags, source_session_id, source_branch, scores, status, created_at, updated_at, recurrence_count). Define Score struct (recurrence, impact, specificity, composite float64). Define EntryStatus (candidate, promoted, archived). See data-model.md for full field definitions.
acceptance_criteria: Types compile and are importable by other packages. All fields from data-model.md are represented.

title: Implement JSONL knowledge store
description: WHY: Knowledge entries need persistent local storage with CRUD operations, following the established issues.jsonl pattern.

items.item: Create, Get, List, Update, Delete methods

design: File: pkg/cli/memory/store.go. Implement Store struct with BasePath. Methods: Create(entry), Get(id), List(filter), Update(id, update), Delete(id), readAll(), writeAll(). Storage path: .specledger/memory/cache/entries.jsonl. Use file locking (gofrs/flock) consistent with issues store. Include filter by status, tags.
acceptance_criteria: CRUD operations work on JSONL file. File locking prevents corruption. Entries persist across CLI invocations.

title: Implement three-axis scorer
description: WHY: Knowledge entries need quality scoring to distinguish high-value patterns from noise.
design: File: pkg/cli/memory/scorer.go. Implement ComputeComposite(score Score) float64 returning average of recurrence, impact, specificity. Implement ShouldPromote(entry) bool checking composite >= 7.0. Define PromotionThreshold constant = 7.0.
acceptance_criteria: Composite score correctly averages three axes. ShouldPromote returns true for scores >= 7.0 and false below.

title: US1: AI Agent Retrieves Knowledge at Session Start
description: WHY: Agents need relevant project knowledge injected into context automatically so users do not have to re-explain past decisions.
design: Implement renderer (knowledge.md from promoted entries), create sl-memory skill for passive context injection, add .specledger/memory/cache/ to .gitignore.

title: Implement knowledge.md renderer from promoted entries
description: WHY: Agent context injection requires a human-readable markdown file generated from promoted entries.

items.item: Empty entries produce empty file gracefully

design: File: pkg/cli/memory/renderer.go. Implement RenderKnowledgeBase(entries []KnowledgeEntry) string. Group entries by tag categories. Output markdown with title, description, source, score. Write to .specledger/memory/knowledge.md.
acceptance_criteria: Promoted entries rendered to well-formatted markdown. Grouped by tag. Includes score and source metadata.

title: Create sl-memory skill for agent context injection
description: WHY: Agents need promoted knowledge automatically loaded as context at session start.
design: File: .claude/skills/sl-memory/skill.md. Skill reads .specledger/memory/knowledge.md and presents it as agent context. Follows same pattern as existing sl-audit and sl-issue-tracking skills.
acceptance_criteria: Skill file exists and references knowledge.md. Agent receives knowledge context when skill is loaded.

title: Add .specledger/memory/cache/ to .gitignore
description: WHY: Cache files should not be committed to the repository.
design: File: .gitignore. Add entry for .specledger/memory/cache/ directory.

title: US2: Extract Knowledge from Session Transcripts
description: WHY: Session transcripts contain valuable patterns, decisions, and debugging insights that need to be extracted into structured knowledge entries.
design: Create /specledger.memory L2 AI command with extraction modes. Create sl memory show CLI command for viewing entries.

title: Create /specledger.memory L2 AI command
description: WHY: Knowledge extraction from unstructured session transcripts requires AI processing to identify patterns, decisions, and conventions.
design: File: .claude/commands/specledger.memory.md. Markdown with instructions for AI agent. Modes: summarize (extract decisions/patterns from a session), tag (assign category tags), patterns (identify recurring patterns across sessions), synthesize (merge related entries). Reads sessions via sl session get, writes entries via sl memory create (future CLI). Output: structured JSON entries with title, description, tags, scores.
acceptance_criteria: AI command file exists with clear instructions. Covers all 4 modes. Output format matches KnowledgeEntry schema.

title: Implement sl memory show CLI command
description: WHY: Users need to view extracted knowledge entries with scores, tags, and status to understand what the system has learned.

items.item: show subcommand registered under memory command
Entries displayed with title, score, status, tags
Output sorted by composite score descending

design: File: pkg/cli/commands/memory.go. Add 'show' subcommand under 'sl memory' that reads entries from JSONL store and displays them in a formatted table. Use existing ui package for formatting. Show: title, composite score, status, tags, source session.
acceptance_criteria: sl memory show displays all knowledge entries with titles, scores, tags, and promotion status. Entries are sorted by composite score descending.

title: US3: Scoring and Promotion
description: Integrate scoring with promotion logic so high-value knowledge entries are automatically promoted when composite score >= 7.0, and users can manually promote/demote entries.
design: Builds on foundational scorer (SL-bdc4d9) and store (SL-47cd52). Add merge/dedup logic to store, create promote/demote CLI commands in pkg/cli/commands/memory.go.

title: US4: View and Manage Knowledge Entries
description: Users need transparency and control over extracted knowledge — ability to delete entries and have the full memory command registered in the CLI.
design: Add delete subcommand to pkg/cli/commands/memory.go. Register VarMemoryCmd in cmd/sl/main.go.

title: US5: Cloud Sync for Knowledge Entries
description: Knowledge entries should be indexed in Supabase for cross-machine and team access, with local cache for offline operation.
design: New file pkg/cli/memory/sync.go for Supabase sync client. Add pull/sync CLI commands. Create Supabase migration for knowledge_entries table.

title: Implement merge/dedup logic in JSONL store
description: WHY: Duplicate knowledge patterns across sessions must be merged, increasing Recurrence score rather than creating separate entries (FR-010).
design: File: pkg/cli/memory/store.go. Add FindSimilar() method that matches entries by title similarity. Add Merge() method that combines entries and increments recurrence_count and recurrence score.
acceptance_criteria: Duplicate entries detected by title similarity are merged. Recurrence score and count are incremented on merge. Original entries are preserved with updated scores.

title: Implement sl memory promote and demote commands
description: WHY: Users must be able to manually promote valuable entries or demote outdated ones regardless of score (FR-005).
design: File: pkg/cli/commands/memory.go. Add promote and demote subcommands. promote sets status to promoted and triggers renderer to regenerate knowledge.md. demote sets status back to candidate and regenerates knowledge.md.
acceptance_criteria: sl memory promote <id> promotes entry and regenerates knowledge.md. sl memory demote <id> demotes entry and regenerates knowledge.md. Auto-promotion at composite >= 7.0 works during extraction.

title: Implement sl memory delete command
description: WHY: Users need to remove unwanted or incorrect knowledge entries entirely (FR-005).

items.item: knowledge.md regenerated if promoted entry deleted

design: File: pkg/cli/commands/memory.go. Add delete subcommand that removes entry from JSONL store and regenerates knowledge.md if entry was promoted.
acceptance_criteria: sl memory delete <id> removes entry from store. If deleted entry was promoted, knowledge.md is regenerated. Confirmation prompt before deletion.

title: Register VarMemoryCmd in main.go
description: WHY: The memory command must be registered in the CLI entry point for users to access it.
design: File: cmd/sl/main.go. Import pkg/cli/commands and add commands.VarMemoryCmd to rootCmd. Follow existing pattern used by VarIssueCmd registration.
acceptance_criteria: sl memory command is accessible from the CLI. All subcommands (show, promote, demote, delete) are available.

title: Implement Supabase sync client for knowledge entries
description: WHY: Knowledge entries need cloud indexing for cross-machine and team access (FR-008).

items.item: Push method uploads local entries to cloud
Pull method downloads cloud entries to local cache

design: File: pkg/cli/memory/sync.go. Create SupabaseSync client that pushes/pulls knowledge entries via PostgREST. Follow existing session metadata sync pattern in pkg/cli/session/. Include project_id and user_id for multi-tenant access.
acceptance_criteria: Sync client can push local entries to Supabase knowledge_entries table. Sync client can pull entries from cloud to local JSONL cache. Offline operation works with sync deferred.

title: Implement sl memory pull and sync commands
description: WHY: Users need CLI commands to sync knowledge with cloud storage (FR-008).
design: File: pkg/cli/commands/memory.go. Add pull subcommand (download from cloud) and sync subcommand (push to cloud). Both use the SupabaseSync client from pkg/cli/memory/sync.go.
acceptance_criteria: sl memory pull downloads entries from cloud to local. sl memory sync pushes local promoted entries to cloud. Both commands report success/failure counts.

title: Create Supabase migration for knowledge_entries table
description: WHY: Cloud storage requires a database table to index knowledge entries with proper schema and access control.

items.item: All KnowledgeEntry fields mapped to columns

design: Create SQL migration file in supabase/migrations/. Table: knowledge_entries with columns: id (UUID PK), project_id, user_id, title, description, tags (text[]), source_session_id, source_branch, score_recurrence, score_impact, score_specificity, composite_score, status, recurrence_count, created_at, updated_at. Add RLS policies for multi-tenant access.
acceptance_criteria: Migration creates knowledge_entries table with all required columns. RLS policies restrict access to project/user scope. Migration is reversible.
